tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Acala, also known as Fudo Myoo, is a powerful Wisdom King in Vajrayana Buddhism.
  • He represents immovable strength and unwavering faith.
  • He protects devotees from evil forces and ignorance.
  • He is often depicted surrounded by flames, symbolizing purification.
  • He plays a key role in spiritual discipline and enlightenment.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Fire Manipulation – Burns away ignorance.
  • Protection – Guards against evil spirits.
  • Discipline – Enforces spiritual focus.
  • Strength – Represents immovable power.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content:
• Widely worshipped in Japanese Buddhism.
  • Known as a fierce but compassionate protector.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Akasagarbha is the Bodhisattva of boundless wisdom and knowledge.
  • His name means “Infinite Space Treasury”.
  • He represents limitless intelligence and memory.
  • He helps seekers gain knowledge and insight.
  • He symbolizes vastness like the sky.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Infinite Knowledge – Grants wisdom.
  • Memory Enhancement – Improves learning.
  • Spiritual Insight – Guides enlightenment.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Akshobhya is one of the Five Dhyani Buddhas.
  • He represents immovable calmness and clarity.
  • He transforms anger into wisdom.
  • He symbolizes mental stability.
  • He is associated with the eastern direction.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Calm Mind – Eliminates anger.
  • Wisdom – Reflects truth like a mirror.
  • Stability – Maintains balance.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Amitabha is the Buddha of Infinite Light.
  • He rules the Pure Land (Sukhavati).
  • He represents compassion and salvation.
  • He guides souls to enlightenment.
  • He is one of the most worshipped Buddhas.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Light – Illuminates truth.
  • Compassion – Saves beings.
  • Guidance – Leads to enlightenment.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Amoghasiddhi represents accomplishment and action.
  • He transforms jealousy into wisdom.
  • He symbolizes fearless action.
  • He is associated with the northern direction.
  • He represents success.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Success – Achieves goals.
  • Action – Drives progress.
  • Fearlessness – Removes doubt.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Avalokiteshvara is the Bodhisattva of compassion.
  • He hears the cries of all beings.
  • He helps relieve suffering.
  • He appears in many forms.
  • He represents kindness.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Compassion – Helps all beings.
  • Healing – Reduces suffering.
  • Transformation – Takes many forms.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Bhaisajyaguru is the Medicine Buddha.
  • He heals physical and mental illness.
  • He represents health and well-being.
  • He removes suffering.
  • He symbolizes healing energy.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Healing – Cures diseases.
  • Medicine – Provides remedies.
  • Protection – Guards health.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Dipankara is a past Buddha.
  • He predicted Gautama Buddha.
  • He represents guidance.
  • He symbolizes continuity.
  • He is part of Buddhist history.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Gandharvas are celestial musicians.
  • They live in heavenly realms.
  • They entertain gods.
  • They symbolize art.
  • They represent sound.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Garuda is a powerful bird-like being.
  • He fights serpents (Nagas).
  • He represents strength and speed.
  • He is a protector figure.
  • He symbolizes freedom.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Gautama Buddha is the founder of Buddhism.
  • He attained enlightenment under the Bodhi tree.
  • He taught the path to end suffering.
  • He represents ultimate wisdom.
  • He symbolizes peace.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Green Tara is a female Bodhisattva of compassion.
  • She protects from fear and danger.
  • She acts quickly to help beings.
  • She represents active compassion.
  • She is widely worshipped.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Hayagriva is a wrathful deity in Vajrayana Buddhism known for fierce protection.
  • He appears with a horse-like head and fiery aura.
  • He destroys obstacles and negative forces.
  • He represents intense compassion in a fierce form.
  • He protects practitioners from spiritual harm.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Protection – Defends against evil forces.
  • Wrathful Energy – Destroys negativity.
  • Spiritual Power – Removes obstacles.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content:
• Common in Tibetan Buddhism.
  • Considered a powerful guardian deity.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Kinnaras are celestial beings known for music and devotion.
  • They are often depicted as half-human, half-bird.
  • They live in heavenly realms.
  • They represent beauty, love, and harmony.
  • They are symbols of devotion.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Music – Divine sound.
  • Harmony – Creates peace.
  • Grace – Symbol of beauty.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Kshitigarbha is the Bodhisattva of compassion for beings in hell realms.
  • He vows to save all suffering souls.
  • He represents patience and kindness.
  • He is often depicted holding a staff.
  • He symbolizes hope for the lost.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Salvation – Helps souls in suffering.
  • Compassion – Deep empathy.
  • Guidance – Leads to liberation.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Kundali is a Wisdom King associated with purification.
  • He uses fire to destroy ignorance.
  • He represents spiritual discipline.
  • He is a fierce protector.
  • He symbolizes transformation.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Mahakala is a powerful protector deity in Vajrayana Buddhism.
  • He is associated with time and destruction of obstacles.
  • He protects teachings and practitioners.
  • He appears in fierce forms.
  • He represents unstoppable force.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Protection – Guards dharma.
  • Destruction – Eliminates enemies.
  • Time Control – Symbolizes time.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Mahasthamaprapta is a Bodhisattva associated with wisdom and power.
  • He is often paired with Amitabha and Avalokiteshvara.
  • He represents spiritual strength.
  • He helps beings achieve enlightenment.
  • He symbolizes inner power.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Manjushri is the Bodhisattva of wisdom.
  • He represents insight and understanding.
  • He cuts ignorance with his sword.
  • He symbolizes intelligence.
  • He is highly respected.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Mara is the embodiment of temptation and illusion.
  • He tries to prevent enlightenment.
  • He challenged Buddha during meditation.
  • He represents inner weakness.
  • He symbolizes desire.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Maitreya is the future Buddha.
  • He will appear to guide humanity.
  • He represents hope.
  • He symbolizes future enlightenment.
  • He is awaited by followers.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Mucalinda is a Naga king who protected Buddha.
  • He sheltered Buddha during a storm.
  • He represents loyalty.
  • He symbolizes protection.
  • He is a sacred serpent figure.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Nagaraja refers to serpent kings in Buddhist mythology.
  • They rule underwater realms.
  • They guard treasures.
  • They represent wisdom.
  • They symbolize hidden knowledge.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Palden Lhamo is a fierce protector goddess.
  • She defends Buddhism.
  • She rides a mule across a sea of blood.
  • She symbolizes fierce compassion.
  • She is a guardian deity.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Ratnasambhava is one of the Five Dhyani Buddhas representing generosity and equality.
  • He transforms pride into wisdom.
  • He symbolizes wealth and abundance.
  • He is associated with the southern direction.
  • He represents balance and fairness.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Generosity – Spreads abundance.
  • Equality – Removes pride.
  • Wisdom – Creates balance.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Samantabhadra is the Bodhisattva of practice and virtue.
  • He represents action and dedication.
  • He is often depicted riding an elephant.
  • He symbolizes moral conduct.
  • He supports enlightenment through practice.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Tara is a compassionate Bodhisattva who protects beings from suffering.
  • She appears in multiple forms.
  • She represents active compassion.
  • She helps overcome fear.
  • She is widely worshipped.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • One of the most important female figures.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Trailokyavijaya is a Wisdom King who conquers evil forces.
  • He represents victory over ignorance.
  • He symbolizes dominance over the three worlds.
  • He appears in fierce form.
  • He protects the dharma.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Vairocana is the central Buddha representing universal truth.
  • He symbolizes cosmic wisdom.
  • He is the source of enlightenment.
  • He represents clarity.
  • He embodies the dharma.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Vajrapani is the protector and power aspect of Buddha.
  • He represents strength.
  • He defends the teachings.
  • He appears in wrathful form.
  • He symbolizes force.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Vajrasattva is associated with purification and cleansing.
  • He removes negative karma.
  • He represents purity.
  • He is central in Vajrayana practices.
  • He symbolizes spiritual cleansing.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Vajrayaksha is a powerful protector spirit.
  • He guards sacred spaces.
  • He represents strength.
  • He is fierce.
  • He protects dharma.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• White Tara is a goddess of healing and long life.
  • She represents peace.
  • She provides protection.
  • She symbolizes purity.
  • She is compassionate.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Yakshas are nature spirits.
  • They guard treasures.
  • They live in forests.
  • They represent nature.
  • They can be good or bad.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Yamantaka is a fierce deity who conquers death.
  • He is a manifestation of Manjushri.
  • He destroys ignorance.
  • He appears terrifying.
  • He symbolizes ultimate victory.
